5b0f2ff37a977fd4b0c845b27726b65682bf8ac6: Differential Privacy (DP) is the gold standard for formal data anonymization in ML, but its real-world adoption is hindered by limited practical guidance and difficulties in balancing privacy, utility, and computation.
Achieving both good accuracy and strong privacy with DP is often possible with sufficient computation and large training datasets.
DP provides formal data anonymization guarantees for training data, distinct from broader privacy principles like transparency or consent.
Rigorous application of DP to complex ML models requires careful attention to architectural adjustments, hyperparameter tuning, and precise privacy accounting, as simple methods may be insufficient.
a68fccc152d238f62848de1be8522ccd71137ac0: Membership inference attacks can be effectively conducted with substantially fewer assumptions than previously, including using only one shadow model or shadow models with different architectures and hyperparameters.
A "data transferring attack" enables effective membership inference even when shadow models are trained on data from entirely different domains.
Membership inference is possible without any shadow models or training, relying solely on statistical analysis of target model posteriors.
Dropout is an effective defense against membership inference for deep neural networks, significantly reducing attack performance while preserving or improving model utility.
Model stacking provides an effective defense mechanism against membership inference for various ML classifiers, substantially reducing attack performance with minimal impact on model utility.
